title: mentha cervina
intro: Mentha cervina, also known as Corn Mint, is a perennial plant belonging to the Lamiaceae family, native to Europe and Western Asia. The plant's leaves and stems contain various bioactive compounds, including menthol, menthone, and limonene, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Mentha cervina has been traditionally used to treat digestive issues, such as indigestion, nausea, and flatulence, due to its carminative and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are commonly consumed to alleviate symptoms and promote overall well-being.
